items.title: Tag Scanning Shall Read Test Files Via AST, Not Compilation
Test-Tag Enforcement Shall Be Opt-In And Configurable Per-Workspace
Declarative Current Truth With Git History
Explicit Subject Ownership For Self-Hosted Internals
File-Backed Linked Strength Requires Literal Ids
Local Skill Scaffold Supports Repo Usage
Spec Tasks Shall Not Start the OTP Application
Command Verification Shall Use Temp-File Output Capture

findings.message: Surface entry does not exist: lib/specled_ex/config.ex
Surface entry does not exist: test/specled_ex/config_test.exs
Surface entry does not exist: lib/specled_ex/tag_scanner.ex
Surface entry does not exist: test/specled_ex/tag_scanner_test.exs

requirements.statement: The repository shall include a .spec/AGENTS.md that tells agents to start a session with mix spec.prime before editing current truth.
The repository shall include a .spec/AGENTS.md that gives local operating guidance for agents working inside the .spec workspace.
The repository shall include a .spec/decisions/README.md that explains when durable ADRs belong in the workspace.
The repository shall include a .spec/README.md that explains purpose, layout, and workflow.

The workspace guidance files shall teach the default local reconcile loop: change the code, tighten the proof, run mix spec.next, update current-truth subjects, use ADRs only for durable cross-cutting policy, and then run mix spec.check --base <ref>.
When indexing and validation run, the workspace shall generate .spec/state.json containing indexed subjects, indexed decisions, and verification state.
Requirements, scenarios, verifications, and exceptions shall validate into Zoi-backed structs with the expected required fields.
The `spec-meta` contract shall accept stable ids, kind, status, optional summary and surface, optional ADR references, and optional verification minimum strength.
Block validation shall reject invalid ids, invalid verification kinds or strengths, and malformed list items with indexed error messages.
The branch guard inside mix spec.check shall fail with an error finding when a cross-cutting change spans multiple impacted subjects without a matching ADR update.

The branch guard inside mix spec.check shall append additive guidance that reports the change type, impacted subjects or uncovered policy files, and the suggested mix spec.next command without changing its enforcement semantics.

When test-tag data is present on the index, the branch guard inside mix spec.check shall emit a finding for each `must` requirement added on the current branch whose id appears in no `@tag spec:` annotation in the configured scan paths.
The branch guard inside mix spec.check shall ignore branch-local planning notes under docs/plans/ when evaluating policy co-changes.

The branch guard inside mix spec.check shall fail when changed code, tests, guides, templates, skills, or governed package files are not matched by current-truth subject spec updates for the impacted subjects, including unmapped changed policy files outside current subject coverage.
The branch guard shall honor `test_tags.enforcement` when promoting new-untagged-requirement findings to error severity.
SpecLedEx.Config shall return the default configuration struct when `.spec/config.yml` is missing, empty, or unreadable, without raising.
mix spec.init shall scaffold a default `.spec/config.yml` alongside the rest of the workspace when one does not already exist.
SpecLedEx.Config shall return defaults when `.spec/config.yml` fails to parse as YAML and shall record a parse diagnostic that the caller can surface.
SpecLedEx.Config shall filter non-string elements out of `test_tags.paths` and fall back to the default paths when filtering yields an empty list.
SpecLedEx.Config shall log a warning and fall back to the default enforcement when `test_tags.enforcement` is present but not `warning` or `error`.

SpecLedEx.Config shall parse `test_tags.enabled` (boolean), `test_tags.paths` (list of strings), and `test_tags.enforcement` (`warning` or `error`) from `.spec/config.yml` and expose them on the returned struct.
ADR files shall require YAML frontmatter with id, status, date, and affects plus Context, Decision, and Consequences sections.
The verifier shall reject ADR affects or supersession links that do not resolve and shall warn when a subject references an unknown ADR id.
State writing shall normalize indexed entities, findings, verification data, and decisions into a canonical `.spec/state.json` artifact with stable ordering and no volatile persisted fields.
JSON state helpers shall return an empty map for missing or invalid files, create parent directories on write, and skip rewriting identical canonical bytes.
Index building shall discover authored subject specs and authored ADRs, detect the canonical workspace directories, and summarize indexed counts without treating `decisions/README.md` as an ADR.
When test-tag scanning is disabled, the `test_tags`, `test_tags_errors`, and `test_tags_config` keys shall be absent or nil on the index so downstream verifiers do not emit tag findings.

Index building shall only scan test tags when enabled by configuration or caller options, and shall store the resulting tag map, parse errors, and effective tag configuration on the index under dedicated keys (`test_tags`, `test_tags_errors`, `test_tags_config`).
mix spec.check shall run indexing, strict validation, and branch-aware co-change enforcement, failing on any errors or warnings.
mix spec.decision.new shall scaffold an ADR under .spec/decisions with the required frontmatter and body sections.
mix spec.index shall build the authored subject and decision index and write .spec/state.json.
In interactive runs, mix spec.init shall offer to scaffold a local Skill for Spec Led Development and write it when the prompt is accepted.
mix spec.init shall create the canonical .spec/ workspace with README.md, AGENTS.md, decisions/README.md, spec_system.spec.md, and package.spec.md.
mix spec.init shall scaffold `.spec/config.yml` alongside the rest of the workspace when one does not already exist.

mix spec.next shall provide a read-only guided reconciliation step that points at the next subject, proof, or ADR update for the current Git change set and tell the maintainer when the branch is ready for the full local check.
No mix spec.* task shall call Mix.Task.run("app.start") or otherwise require the host OTP application to be running, since spec tasks perform only file I/O, Git CLI calls, and in-memory parsing.

mix spec.prime shall provide a read-only session-start summary that combines workspace status, current-branch guidance, and the default local loop without writing current-truth files or derived state.
mix spec.prime shall support JSON output for agent consumption and shall only execute eligible command verifications when --run-commands is passed.
mix spec.status shall summarize coverage, verification strength, weak spots, and ADR usage for the current workspace, executing command verifications by default unless explicitly opted out.
mix spec.check and mix spec.validate shall accept `--test-tags` and `--no-test-tags` flags that enable or disable test-tag scanning for that invocation, overriding the config default.
When building the index, the effective test-tag enabled value shall follow CLI flag > `.spec/config.yml` > built-in default precedence.
mix spec.validate shall exit non-zero whenever the generated verification report status is fail.
mix spec.validate shall validate specs, derive findings, and write .spec/state.json with a verification report before returning.
mix spec.next --bugfix shall prefer regression proof first and direct the maintainer to confirm whether the current subject wording already captures the fix before adding new spec churn.

mix spec.next shall inspect the current Git change set, classify it as covered local, covered cross-cutting, uncovered frontier, or likely non-contract, and list the impacted subjects or uncovered policy files plus the next suggested commands.
mix spec.next shall stay read-only and return guidance output without writing current-truth files or derived state.
mix spec.next shall report whether the branch is ready for check, still needs subject updates, still needs a durable ADR update, or needs a new subject.
The package shall keep `.spec` declarative and current-state only, using ADRs for durable cross-cutting policy and Git history for change over time.

The package README shall teach mix spec.prime as the session-start context command, a default local loop centered on mix spec.next and mix spec.check, explain the ready-for-check decision, reserve ADRs for durable cross-cutting policy, and present mix spec.status as occasional plus mix spec.index and mix spec.validate as advanced plumbing.
The package shall index authored subject specs, index durable ADRs, and write derived state to .spec/state.json.
The package shall provide mix spec.init, mix spec.prime, mix spec.next, mix spec.check, mix spec.status, mix spec.decision.new, mix spec.index, and mix spec.validate as user-facing commands.
The parser shall continue parsing and collect parse errors when a block cannot be decoded, when a structured block appears more than once, or when block items fail schema validation.
The parser shall extract spec-meta, spec-requirements, spec-scenarios, spec-verification, and spec-exceptions fenced blocks from a spec file.
The parser shall record the first Markdown H1 heading as the subject title.
mix spec.prime shall keep command verification execution off by default and only execute eligible command verifications when --run-commands is passed.
mix spec.prime shall support JSON output that nests the workspace status report, current-branch guidance, and loop steps for agent consumption.
mix spec.prime shall provide a read-only session-start summary that combines workspace status, current-branch guidance, and the default local loop for the current repository.
mix spec.status shall summarize source, guide, and test coverage plus weak spots by subject from the current workspace, using executed command proof by default unless explicitly disabled.
state output and mix spec.status shall summarize indexed ADRs and subject-to-ADR references.
mix spec.status shall include frontier data for uncovered source, guide, and test files plus short next-gap hints for brownfield adoption.
The tag scanner shall deduplicate identical file, line, and test_name entries under the same requirement id.

The tag scanner shall detect `@tag spec` annotations whose value is not a string or list literal and report them as a separate collection that the verifier can emit as tag_dynamic_value_skipped findings.
The tag scanner shall ignore `@tag` annotations that do not carry a `spec` key.
A `@moduletag spec` annotation shall attach its requirement id to every test defined in that module.
The tag scanner shall collect each unparseable file into the parse_errors list instead of silently skipping it, so the verifier can emit a tag_scan_parse_error finding.
SpecLedEx.TagScanner.scan/2 shall return an ok tuple carrying a tag_map of requirement id to list of test occurrences and a parse_errors list of file/reason entries aggregated across the input paths.

The tag scanner shall extract requirement ids from the four supported forms (`@tag spec` with a string, `@tag` with a keyword list containing a `spec` entry, `@tag spec` with a list of strings, and `@moduletag spec`) without compiling the test files.

Command verifications shall capture output via temp files and wait for process exit status, not pipe EOF. Commands shall run under a configurable timeout (default 120s). Output and exit code files shall be cleaned up even if reading fails.
Verification shall warn when a requirement has no covering verification entry.
Verification shall validate ADR frontmatter, required ADR sections, ADR affects references, supersession links, and subject decision references.
Verification shall ignore malformed non-map parsed block entries, rely on recorded parse errors, and continue producing a report.
Verification shall emit errors when a subject is missing spec-meta id, kind, or status.
Verification shall detect duplicate subject ids, duplicate requirement ids, unresolved scenario covers, and unresolved verification covers.

When test-tag data is present on the index, verification shall emit a `requirement_without_test_tag` finding for each `must` requirement whose id appears in no `@tag spec:` annotation within the configured scan paths.
Verification strength ordering shall treat `claimed`, `linked`, and `executed` as an ordered proof scale and use that ordering to enforce effective minimum strength thresholds.
Verification shall emit a `tag_dynamic_value_skipped` finding for each `@tag spec:` annotation whose value the tag scanner could not resolve to a literal string or list of strings.
The severity of `requirement_without_test_tag` and `verification_cover_untagged` findings shall follow the configured `test_tags.enforcement` value (`warning` or `error`), defaulting to warning.
Verification shall not emit any tag-related findings when the index has no test-tag data.
Verification shall emit a `tag_scan_parse_error` finding (severity warning) for each file that the tag scanner recorded as unparseable.
Verification shall warn when a file-based verification target does not exist on disk.

When test-tag data is present on the index, verification shall emit a `verification_cover_untagged` finding for each `test_file` or `test` verification whose `covers:` ids are not backed by a matching `@tag spec:` annotation in the referenced file.

given: the same new untagged requirement as above
a config that sets test_tags.enforcement to :error
then: the finding severity is error
mix spec.check exits non-zero

given: a branch that adds a new `must` requirement `billing.invoice` to an existing subject
a tag_map that does not contain `billing.invoice`
then: a finding is produced naming `billing.invoice` and the file where it was added
when: mix spec.check runs against the branch base

given: a fresh workspace without `.spec/config.yml`
then: `.spec/config.yml` exists
the scaffolded YAML parses back to defaults via SpecLedEx.Config.load/2

given: a `.spec/config.yml` that sets test_tags.enabled to true, test_tags.paths to ["test/specled_ex"], and test_tags.enforcement to error
then: the struct has test_tags.enabled true
the struct has test_tags.paths equal to ["test/specled_ex"]
the struct has test_tags.enforcement equal to :error

given: a `.spec/config.yml` that is not valid YAML
then: the returned config equals SpecLedEx.Config.defaults/0
a parse diagnostic is recorded on the returned result

given: a workspace with no `.spec/config.yml`
then: the returned config equals SpecLedEx.Config.defaults/0

given: a `.spec/config.yml` with test_tags.paths containing ["test", 42, null]
then: the struct test_tags.paths equals ["test"]

given: a `.spec/config.yml` that sets test_tags.enforcement to the string catastrophic
then: the struct test_tags.enforcement equals the default value
a Logger.warning is emitted naming the unknown value

given: a workspace with test-tag scanning disabled
then: the index has `test_tags`, `test_tags_errors`, and `test_tags_config` absent or nil

given: a workspace whose `.spec/config.yml` enables test_tags
a test file containing an `@tag spec` annotation with id a.one
then: the index has a non-nil `test_tags` map containing `a.one`
the index has a `test_tags_config` entry describing the effective config

given: a spec file with two empty spec-requirements blocks
then: the result includes a duplicate-block parse error
the parser does not crash

given: a spec file with invalid JSON in a spec-meta block
then: the result includes a parse error
the parser does not crash

given: a test file containing two identical `@tag spec` annotations with id `a.one` on the same test
then: `a.one` appears exactly once in the returned list

given: a test file containing a keyword-list `@tag` annotation whose `spec` entry is `auth.logout` and whose `timeout` entry is 5_000
then: the returned tag list contains `auth.logout`
the `timeout` key is ignored

given: a test file containing an `@tag spec` annotation whose value is the list `[a.one, a.two]`
then: the returned tag list contains both `a.one` and `a.two` linked to the same test

given: a test file containing an `@tag spec` annotation with the string literal `auth.login` before a `test/2` definition
then: the returned tag list contains `auth.login` linked to that test name

given: a test file declaring a `@moduletag spec` annotation with id `domain.root` at the top
the module contains two `test/2` blocks
then: `domain.root` is linked to both test names

given: a test file containing an `@tag spec` annotation whose value is a module attribute reference
then: the file is reported in the dynamic-value collection with its file path and line
no requirement id is added to the tag_map for that test

given: a test file containing an `@tag` annotation whose value is the atom `:slow` and no `spec` key
then: the returned tag list is empty

given: a test file that cannot be parsed as Elixir
then: the parse_errors list contains an entry with that file path and a reason
scanning the remaining files still produces a tag_map
when: SpecLedEx.TagScanner.scan/2 runs on a directory containing it

given: an indexed subject containing malformed parsed block entries and parse errors
then: the report includes the parse error finding
verification does not crash

given: a spec with a source_file verification pointing to a path that does not exist
then: a warning finding is produced for the missing target

given: a spec with a command verification targeting "printf ok" with execute true
then: the command output is captured correctly
the exit code is 0
no temp files remain after verification

given: a spec with a command verification targeting "exit 2"
then: the exit code is 2
the verification is reported as failed

given: a spec with a command verification targeting a slow command
then: the command is killed after the timeout
a non-zero exit code is recorded
when: verification runs with run_commands true and a short timeout

given: a config that sets test_tags.enforcement to :error
a must requirement that has no backing `@tag spec` annotation
then: the `requirement_without_test_tag` finding is reported with severity error

given: an index with no `test_tags` key
then: no `requirement_without_test_tag`, `verification_cover_untagged`, `tag_scan_parse_error`, or `tag_dynamic_value_skipped` finding is emitted

given: an index with a `must` requirement `billing.invoice` and a tag_map that does not contain `billing.invoice`
then: a `requirement_without_test_tag` finding is emitted for `billing.invoice`

given: an index with a test_tags_errors entry for file test/broken.exs and any reason
then: a warning `tag_scan_parse_error` finding is emitted referencing `test/broken.exs`

given: a test_file verification targeting test/billing_test.exs that covers billing.invoice
a tag_map showing no `@tag spec` annotation for billing.invoice in test/billing_test.exs
then: a `verification_cover_untagged` finding is emitted for `billing.invoice` on that file

given: a spec with a requirement that has no covering verification
then: a warning finding is produced for the uncovered requirement

meta.summary: Validates authored block payloads into Zoi-backed structs with precise validation errors.
Uses the current Git change set to enforce subject co-changes and cross-cutting ADR updates during the final local check.
Loads `.spec/config.yml` with defaults and exposes test-tag scanning settings to the index, verifier, and CLI tasks.
Parses ADRs, validates their contract, and lets subject specs reference durable cross-cutting decisions.
Builds the authored index and writes canonical derived state for the workspace.
Mix tasks for scaffolding, session-start priming, indexing, guiding, validating, summarizing, and strictly checking Spec Led Development workspaces.
Classifies the current change set, points at impacted subjects or uncovered frontier files, and suggests the next Spec Led step without writing current truth automatically.
Elixir package for Spec Led Development. Provides Mix tasks to scaffold, orient, index, guide, validate, summarize, and strictly check authored specs.
Extracts structured spec blocks from authored Markdown files.
Combines workspace status, current-branch guidance, and the default local loop into one session-start command.
Canonical workspace contract for authored specs and generated Spec Led state.
Builds current-state summaries for coverage, verification strength, weak spots, and ADR usage.
AST-based scanner that extracts tag spec values from ExUnit files without compiling them.
Validates authored specs, checks references, derives findings, and writes state.

verifications.target: mix test test/mix/tasks/spec_tasks_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_status_task_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_next_task_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_prime_task_test.exs

mix run -e 'Mix.Task.load_all(); Enum.each(~w(spec.init spec.prime spec.next spec.check spec.status spec.decision.new spec.index spec.validate), fn task -> Mix.Task.get(task) || raise("missing #{task}") end)'
